stem: An analyst modeled the demand Q for a product as a linear function of selling price P. The demand was 30,000 units at $50 per unit and 18,000 units at $80 per unit. Based on this model, what is the demand, in units, at a selling price of $70 per unit?
rationale:
Choice C is correct. The slope is (18000 - 30000)/(80 - 50) = -12000/30 = -400 units per dollar. Using point (50, 30000): Q = 30000 - 400(P - 50). At P = 70: Q = 30000 - 400(20) = 30000 - 8000 = 22,000 units.

Choices A, B, and D arise from arithmetic mistakes or incorrect slope computation.
